description: Data curation profile. Schema-compliance and consistency are primary quality gates. Suited for content classification, dataset cleaning, taxonomy building, and structured output preparation.

bootstrap.description: Read the saga task.json so the worker knows the curation goal and current stage.
Read progress.json to see which items have already been processed and validated.
Read the most recent checkpoint to resume batch processing context.

hard.title: Schema compliance
hard.passDescription: every output record validates against the declared schema
hard.failReworkDescription: some records have schema violations that can be fixed
hard.failEscalateDescription: the declared schema is contradictory or the source data cannot fit the schema

hard.title: Coverage
hard.passDescription: outputCount / inputCount ≥ declaredThreshold (default 0.95 unless filtering was explicitly requested)
hard.failReworkDescription: coverage is below threshold but more items can be processed
hard.failEscalateDescription: source data is too incomplete or corrupt to reach the coverage threshold

hard.title: Subjective scores calibrated
hard.passDescription: for any 1–5 score field, spot-checks show scores are not all clumped (e.g., not 90% scored 3)
hard.failReworkDescription: score distribution is skewed; recalibration in rework can fix it
hard.failEscalateDescription: the scoring criteria are inherently subjective and cannot be made consistent

soft.title: Subjective-score consistency
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = scores follow rubric consistently; 1 = random-seeming scores

soft.title: Coverage
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = all scoped items processed; 1 = major gaps

soft.title: Output organization
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = well-structured, easy to navigate; 1 = disorganized dump

criteria.description: All items in scope have been processed and appear in the output.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = 100% of scoped items processed; 1 = major gaps in coverage.

criteria.description: Similar items are categorised/labelled consistently throughout the dataset.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = no inconsistencies found; 1 = same item type labelled differently in multiple places.

criteria.description: All output records conform to the declared output schema.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = zero schema violations; 1 = widespread schema violations.

criteria.description: All declared categories/tags have been applied where appropriate; no under-coverage.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = all categories well-represented; 1 = major categories missing.

criteria.description: The curation report is clear and a successor worker can understand decisions made.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = decision rationale documented throughout; 1 = opaque categorisation.

benchGoal.goal: Classify 5 product descriptions into categories (electronics, clothing, food, books) and write results to categories.json.
